calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jul-30-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances; Timothy Chambers (present in Court) for and with Marsha McLaulin (Administrator) appeared by Zoom.

The Court questions whether the cash of this estate is still being held out of State. Mr. Chambers stated that the cash is still held out of State in a bank in Florida, that he thinks he has a solution, and that he has a declaration from Counsel for the Bank of Central Florida indicating that the bank has no issue with accepting the validity of the letters that were issued to his Client and no problem with accepting instruction from his Client as to how the assets are to be distributed. Mr. Chambers also stated that if his Client were to be required to open a bank account in California, she would have to fly to California to open the account, that he has a letter from his Client's Doctor regarding his Client's health condition and indicating that she is unable to travel due to the COVID pandemic health risks, and that there are 39 beneficiaries spread out all over the United States. In addition, Mr. Chambers spoke of the CEB/California Civil Code that provides this Court with authority to direct how assets held in an estate account are to be distributed from accounts outside of the State of California that are deemed to be located in the account owner's domicile. Mr. Chambers also stated that he is hopeful that the Court will accept the declarations and move this forward, and provided courtesy copies of two declarations and a proposed order. The Court granted the Second Amended First and Final Report of Administrator and Petition for Its Settlement, etc. (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Court Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Deputy: C. Cabasares. Not reported.

Petition For Approval And Authorization Of Settlement Agreement
Off Calendar Jul-02-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Timothy Chambers for Marsha McLaulin (Administrator).
Alan Sparer for Winterthur Musuem.

Mr. Chambers responded to the Examiner's position that the Administrator has no capacity to execute a settlement agreement with Winterthur regarding interpretation of the will, asked the Court to sign an order regarding entitlement to the estate, spoke of providing two orders, one for the settlement agreement and one for entitlement, spoke of what would happen if they had to start over and asked to have the final accounting continued. Mr. Chambers and Mr. Sparer requested that the Court approve the settlement agreement. The Petition for Approval of Settlement Agreement is under submission. The issue of entitlement to estate is bifurcated and under submission. The Amended First and Final Account, etc. is continued to August 5, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Sparer reviewed the proposed orders and approved them as to form.

Judge: Andrew Y. S. Cheng. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

Off Calendar Nov-21-2016 Continued To Dec-12-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

An Order to Appear will be issued to the administrator Marsha McLaulin and the Attorney Timothy Chambers to appear on December 12, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why the declaration on date of death of Mr. McDaniels has not been filed. The status hearing is also continued to that date.

Judge: Paul Alvarado. Clerk: Leslie Gomez/Yolanda Mazariegos. Bailiff: Deputy Ideta. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Sep-08-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Timothy Chambers for (proposed executor) Marsha McLaulin.

The Court asked about Heirship.com potentially confirming Mr. McDaniels death. Mr. Chambers stated that they located Mr. McDaniels sister who confirms that he passed away in Hawaii but could not confirm the date and that they are still working on the date. The Court granted the petition for letters of administration with will annexed (subject to review of the order) and set a status date for a declaration confirming the date of death of Mr. McDaniels on November 21, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Fender. (Not Reported)
